methods.name: shopify.graphql.query
methods.description: Execute a Shopify Admin GraphQL query document against the pinned provider schema.

notes: Use this method for long-tail Shopify fields that do not justify a dedicated convenience alias.
Prefer a named operation and request only the fields you actually need.
For connection fields, include pageInfo and cursors explicitly in the document.
Inspect extensions.cost.throttleStatus to understand current GraphQL budget and restore rate.

example_payload.document: query ShopIdentity { shop { id name myshopifyDomain primaryDomain { host url } } }

methods.name: shopify.graphql.mutate
methods.description: Execute a Shopify Admin GraphQL mutation document against the pinned provider schema.

notes: Use this method for provider-native Shopify mutations when there is no dedicated convenience alias.
Treat mutations as bounded and deliberate; inspect userErrors before assuming success.
Prefer cleanup-safe probes and reversible operational actions when validating write posture.

example_payload.document: mutation BulkProbe($query: String!) { bulkOperationRunQuery(query: $query) { bulkOperation { id status type } userErrors { field message } } }

methods.name: shopify.query.shop
methods.description: Read Shopify shop from the Admin GraphQL shop query field.

notes: Use for shop identity, primary domain, and high-level storefront metadata.

methods.name: shopify.query.orders
methods.description: Read Shopify orders from the Admin GraphQL orders query field.
selection: edges { cursor node { id name createdAt updatedAt } }

default_selection: edges { cursor node { id name createdAt updatedAt } }
notes: Prefer sortKey and cursors for repeatable pagination through large order histories.
Use the query argument for bounded searches instead of pulling the whole order set.

methods.name: shopify.query.order
methods.description: Read one Shopify order from the Admin GraphQL order query field.
selection: lineItems(first: 25) { edges { cursor node { id name quantity sku } } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } }

default_selection: lineItems(first: 25) { edges { cursor node { id name quantity sku } } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } }
notes: Use the gid form gid://shopify/Order/<id> when reading one order.

methods.name: shopify.query.products
methods.description: Read Shopify products from the Admin GraphQL products query field.
selection: edges { cursor node { id title handle updatedAt } }

default_selection: edges { cursor node { id title handle updatedAt } }
notes: Prefer query and cursors for bounded product scans.
Add inventory or publication fields explicitly when those details matter.

methods.name: shopify.query.product
methods.description: Read one Shopify product from the Admin GraphQL product query field.
selection: variants(first: 25) { edges { node { id title sku } } }

default_selection: variants(first: 25) { edges { node { id title sku } } }
notes: Use the gid form gid://shopify/Product/<id> when reading one product.

methods.name: shopify.query.customers
methods.description: Read Shopify customers from the Admin GraphQL customers query field.
selection: edges { cursor node { id displayName email updatedAt } }

default_selection: edges { cursor node { id displayName email updatedAt } }
notes: Customer queries can be large; prefer first plus cursors and narrow searches.

methods.name: shopify.query.customer
methods.description: Read one Shopify customer from the Admin GraphQL customer query field.

notes: Use the gid form gid://shopify/Customer/<id> when reading one customer.
